focus: Test infrastructure, benchmark harness depth, PR 9 baseline, PR 5 cardinality recheck, cross-PR test breakage, and adversarial validation of iteration-4 findings

title: Benchmark files are now on the default test path and one parse fixture depends on untracked build output
reproduce: On a clean checkout without generated mcp_server/dist/startup-checks.js, run npm run test:core --workspace=@spec-kit/mcp-server or vitest with mcp_server/vitest.config.ts; the default include reaches code-graph-parse-latency.bench.ts and asserts the untracked dist fixture exists.
fix: Remove bench/**/*.bench.ts from the default include and add an explicit bench config/script, or replace the dist fixture with a tracked source fixture and document build order if default inclusion is intentional.

title: Benchmark suites force SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED=true and do not restore the prior environment
reproduce: Call runQueryLatencyBench() or run the bench files before a regular test that expects metrics to remain disabled; isSpeckitMetricsEnabled() observes the benchmark's forced true unless the caller manually restores process.env.SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED.
fix: Snapshot and restore the previous SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED value in every bench afterAll/finally block, paired with speckitMetrics.reset(); consider vi.stubEnv/vi.unstubAllEnvs for Vitest consistency.

title: The signal/noise bench only asserts signal presence and manually emits the metric it claims to verify
reproduce: Change renderAdvisorBrief() so noisy non-live renderables still return text, or remove production recommendation metric emission outside the bench; the current bench can still pass because it checks only positive cases and increments the counter itself.
fix: Add negative/noise fixtures that assert null output, and drive the production path for metric emission or rename the assertion to collector-shape coverage.

evidence: Closed-label cardinality is roughly 126 + 6 * skill_count, so 200 skills is about 1326 series rather than over 9000; however skill_id remains unbounded repo metadata and SpeckitMetricsCollector still directly keys labels in metrics.ts:578-590 with no runtime guard.

The reviewed settings commands avoid direct interpolation but still resolve hook targets from ambient git root/pwd and unqualified node; no reviewed code anchors execution to the trusted settings-file directory.
The baseline math catches a 10x p50 regression, but runQueryLatencyBench() still converts setup exceptions into skipped passing reports at code-graph-query-latency.bench.ts:153-168.
Focused Vitest run passed 5 files and 31 tests, including code-graph-context-handler.vitest.ts, code-graph-query-handler.vitest.ts, and all three bench files.
